items.title: Shannon entropy
items.description: D_KL(P || Q) = H(P, Q) − H(P). Entropy H(P) is the irreducible uncertainty in P; KL is the additional cost of encoding P with Q.

items.title: Cross-entropy
items.description: Cross-entropy H(P, Q) decomposes into H(P) + D_KL(P || Q). Minimizing cross-entropy on fixed P is equivalent to minimizing KL to P.

items.title: Maximum likelihood estimation
items.description: Maximizing average log-likelihood on empirical data is equivalent to minimizing D_KL(P̂ || Q_θ) where P̂ is the empirical distribution and Q_θ is the parametric model.

items.title: Variational inference and ELBO
items.description: The ELBO in VAEs and variational inference contains KL terms between approximate and true posteriors (or priors), driving q to stay close to p in KL sense.

items.title: Mutual information
items.description: Mutual information can be written using KL between joint and product of marginals: I(X; Y) = D_KL(P_{XY} || P_X P_Y).
